title: Chapter 1 Quiz
description: Test your understanding of React foundations!

questions.question: What is React?
options: A JavaScript framework for building backends
A JavaScript library for building user interfaces
questions.explanation: React is a library, not a full framework, and it focuses on UI.

questions.question: React components must always return JSX.
questions.explanation: Components can return other things like strings, numbers, or arrays, but typically they return JSX.

questions.question: Which command creates a new React app with Create React App?
questions.explanation: npx create-react-app my-app is the correct command.

questions.question: The Virtual DOM is a direct copy of the browser's DOM.
questions.explanation: It's a lightweight JavaScript representation, not an exact copy.

questions.question: What does a functional component look like?
questions.explanation: A functional component is a plain JavaScript function that returns JSX.

questions.question: What does JSX stand for?
questions.explanation: JSX is a syntax extension that lets you write HTML-like markup in JavaScript.

questions.question: Create React App is the only way to set up a React project.
questions.explanation: You can also use Vite, Next.js, or set up manually, but CRA is the simplest for beginners.

questions.question: React components are like reusable _____ that you combine to build UIs.
questions.explanation: Components are often functions (functional components) that return UI.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of the Virtual DOM?
options: It makes React run on the server
questions.explanation: By working with a lightweight copy, React can batch updates and avoid costly direct DOM changes.

questions.question: You need to install Node.js to use Create React App.
questions.explanation: Create React App requires Node.js because it uses npm (or yarn) to manage dependencies.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about React?
options: It is a complete framework with built-in routing
It can be used with React Native for mobile apps
questions.explanation: React is a library, not a full framework. Routing is handled by separate libraries like React Router.

questions.question: A React component can only return a single root element.
questions.explanation: JSX expressions must have one parent element. You can use <>...</> (fragments) to group without adding extra nodes.

questions.question: The process of comparing the new Virtual DOM with the previous one is called _____.
questions.explanation: React uses a diffing algorithm to find what changed.

questions.question: What does the `npx` command do?
options: Executes a package without installing it permanently
questions.explanation: npx runs a package binary from the npm registry without global installation.

questions.question: React components can only be written as functions, not as classes.
questions.explanation: Originally components were written as classes, but with hooks, functional components are now the standard. Both are possible.

questions.question: What is the default port for a Create React App development server?
questions.explanation: CRA starts the dev server on port 3000 by default.

questions.question: JSX is required to use React.
questions.explanation: You can use React without JSX by using `React.createElement`, but JSX is much more convenient.

questions.question: Which file is the entry point of a typical Create React App?
questions.explanation: index.js is where ReactDOM renders the App component into the DOM.

questions.question: React can only run in the browser.
questions.explanation: React can also render on the server (Next.js) and in mobile apps (React Native).

questions.question: To update the UI in React, you change the component's _____.
questions.explanation: State holds data that may change over time; when state updates, the component re-renders.
